replies.msg:
Cathay Pacific updates to media (10 August)

國泰航空最新資訊 (8月10 日)

1. 我們收到乘客及公眾就我們一名被控暴動罪的機師的查詢。根據我們決定機師飛行職務的標準程序，該機師自2019年7月30日起已沒有被安排飛行職務。此外，他自2019年7月15日起亦未執行任何飛行任務。國泰航空明確表示我們對於他將可能受到任何的訴訟事宜，均不發表任何意見。一如既往，我們一直視運作安全及保安為首要考量。

2. 我們證實兩名機場員工因行為不當已被解僱。

【內部電郵】

10 August 2019
Important Announcement￼

Dear colleagues,

As a global airline group headquartered in the HKSAR, we are required and indeed we are committed to following all the applicable aviation regulations set down both in Hong Kong and in the countries to which we fly, or over which we fly.

Last night, the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) issued a notice to the Cathay Pacific Group informing us of new conditions we are required to fulfil. These include:

Effective midnight Saturday, 10 August 2019, Cathay Pacific Group employees who support or take part in illegal protests, violent actions, or overly radical behaviour shall be immediately suspended from any activity involving flights to the Mainland and from any activities related to air transportation pertaining to the Mainland.

Effective midnight Sunday, 11 August 2019, the Cathay Pacific Group shall submit identification details of all crews flying to/overflying Mainland airspace to the CAAC for approval. Flights without approval, prior to departure, will not be given permission to land or overfly.

Before midnight 15 August 2019, the Cathay Pacific Group shall report to the CAAC providing details of action taken to step up internal safety controls and improve flight safety and security.

We share CAAC’s views about the paramount importance of flight safety.

Cathay Pacific Group’s operations in mainland China are key to our business. In addition to flying in and out of mainland China, a large number of our routes both to Europe and to the USA also fly through mainland China airspace. We are therefore legally required to follow CAAC regulations and, as is the case with any notices issued by any regulatory authority having jurisdiction over us, we must and will comply. This means that in order for our employees to perform their duties, they would need to satisfy the requirements as set out in the notice above.

There have been a number of incidents over the past few weeks, some involving our employees, which have placed the Cathay Pacific Group in the spotlight. Our primary focus must remain on delivering a safe, comfortable customer experience for everyone who chooses to fly with us. At the same time, we always try to create a safe, supportive environment for all Cathay Pacific Group employees.

Though people may share different views, it is essential that we all respect each other, our customers and members of the public. We have zero tolerance for inappropriate behaviour that affects the safe operation of our flights and the service experience we provide to our customers. We also have zero tolerance for any act which constitutes cyber-bulling, breaches our Corporate Code of Conduct or our Social Media Policy.

We are aware that this has been, and continues to be, a very challenging time for many of us who live in Hong Kong and call this incredible city home. We trust all of our employees will continue to be the service professionals they have always been – and to continue to support one another and to respect one another, inside and outside of work, as we move forward.

We will continue to communicate with everyone to keep you updated on any changes to the business circumstances we are facing.

If you have further questions, please do speak with your GM / RGM or Director.

Rupert

國泰證實兩名機場員工因行為不當已被解僱
2019-08-10 HKT 20:45

國泰航空表示，上月30日起已沒向被控暴動罪的機師安排飛行職務；他自上月15日起亦已沒執行飛行職務，並證實兩名機場員工因行為不當已被解僱。

民航局早前向國泰發出重大航空安全風險警示，並提出三點要求。根據國泰航空行政總裁致員工的內部訊息，來往內地的航班對國泰業務十分重要，亦有不少途經內地空域，國泰需要遵從民航局的規管，員工為執行職務，需要滿足民航局的要求。

內部訊息強調，首要考量仍然是為旅客提供安全舒適的體驗，國泰對影響航班安全運作和服務的不適當行為，採取零容忍態度。

https://news.rthk.hk/rthk/ch/component/k2/1473860-20190810.htm

quote.msg:
Cathay Pacific updates to media (10 August)

國泰航空最新資訊 (8月10 日)

1. 我們收到乘客及公眾就我們一名被控暴動罪的機師的查詢。根據我們決定機師飛行職務的標準程序，該機師自2019年7月30日起已沒有被安排飛行職務。此外，他自2019年7月15日起亦未執行任何飛行任務。國泰航空明確表示我們對於他將可能受到任何的訴訟事宜，均不發表任何意見。一如既往，我們一直視運作安全及保安為首要考量。

2. 我們證實兩名機場員工因行為不當已被解僱。

【內部電郵】

10 August 2019
Important Announcement￼

Dear colleagues,

As a global airline group headquartered in the HKSAR, we are required and indeed we are committed to following all the applicable aviation regulations set down both in Hong Kong and in the countries to which we fly, or over which we fly.

Last night, the Civil Aviation Administration of China (CAAC) issued a notice to the Cathay Pacific Group informing us of new conditions we are required to fulfil. These include:

Effective midnight Saturday, 10 August 2019, Cathay Pacific Group employees who support or take part in illegal protests, violent actions, or overly radical behaviour shall be immediately suspended from any activity involving flights to the Mainland and from any activities related to air transportation pertaining to the Mainland.

Effective midnight Sunday, 11 August 2019, the Cathay Pacific Group shall submit identification details of all crews flying to/overflying Mainland airspace to the CAAC for approval. Flights without approval, prior to departure, will not be given permission to land or overfly.

Before midnight 15 August 2019, the Cathay Pacific Group shall report to the CAAC providing details of action taken to step up internal safety controls and improve flight safety and security.

We share CAAC’s views about the paramount importance of flight safety.

Cathay Pacific Group’s operations in mainland China are key to our business. In addition to flying in and out of mainland China, a large number of our routes both to Europe and to the USA also fly through mainland China airspace. We are therefore legally required to follow CAAC regulations and, as is the case with any notices issued by any regulatory authority having jurisdiction over us, we must and will comply. This means that in order for our employees to perform their duties, they would need to satisfy the requirements as set out in the notice above.

There have been a number of incidents over the past few weeks, some involving our employees, which have placed the Cathay Pacific Group in the spotlight. Our primary focus must remain on delivering a safe, comfortable customer experience for everyone who chooses to fly with us. At the same time, we always try to create a safe, supportive environment for all Cathay Pacific Group employees.

Though people may share different views, it is essential that we all respect each other, our customers and members of the public. We have zero tolerance for inappropriate behaviour that affects the safe operation of our flights and the service experience we provide to our customers. We also have zero tolerance for any act which constitutes cyber-bulling, breaches our Corporate Code of Conduct or our Social Media Policy.

We are aware that this has been, and continues to be, a very challenging time for many of us who live in Hong Kong and call this incredible city home. We trust all of our employees will continue to be the service professionals they have always been – and to continue to support one another and to respect one another, inside and outside of work, as we move forward.

We will continue to communicate with everyone to keep you updated on any changes to the business circumstances we are facing.

If you have further questions, please do speak with your GM / RGM or Director.

Rupert
